seoTitle: Point Lookout vs Point Vernon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Lookout and Point Vernon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Lookout vs Point Vernon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Lookout and Point Vernon.
heading: Point Lookout vs Point Vernon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Lookout, Queensland and Point Vernon, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Vernon has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to Point Lookout (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Lookout or Point Vernon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Lookout has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Point Vernon scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Lookout scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Point Vernon scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Lookout or Point Vernon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Vernon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
